root.narration: You just won the first two points of a game. Your friend asks what the score is. You are serving. What do you call out?

choices.text: 30-love! In tennis, the first point is 15, the second is 30, and love means zero
2-0, I am winning by two points
I have no idea, I just keep hitting the ball

outcome.headline: You know your tennis scoring!
outcome.explanation: Tennis uses a special scoring system: love, 15, 30, 40, game. The server always says their score first. You are ahead 30-love, which means you only need two more points to win this game.
outcome.whatToRemember: Tennis points go love, 15, 30, 40, game. The server always announces their score first.

keyTerms.definition: The tennis word for zero. When someone has not scored any points yet, their score is called love

outcome.headline: Right idea, wrong words!
outcome.explanation: You know you are ahead, but tennis does not use regular numbers. Instead of 1, 2, 3 it goes 15, 30, 40. Learning the lingo is part of the game.
outcome.whatToRemember: Tennis has its own special scoring words. Practice saying them: love, 15, 30, 40, deuce, advantage, game.

outcome.headline: You need to track the score!
outcome.explanation: In tennis, players are responsible for knowing the score, especially when serving. If nobody keeps score, you will not know who won the game.
outcome.whatToRemember: Always keep track of the score and call it out loud before each serve. It keeps the match fair and organized.
